line: - `is_bash_failure()` across the 4 failure signal types plus a success case
- `compute_io_rate()` retained from the helper set while already in scope
- `TokenTracker::is_frozen()` can be tested through public API by constructing trackers with past `Instant`s, avoiding access to private helper methods.

items: `TokenTracker::is_frozen()` can be tested through public API by constructing trackers with past `Instant`s, avoiding access to private helper methods.

line: - `compute_silence()` intentionally prioritizes visible activity sources (`last_jsonl_record_at`, `last_file_event_at`, `last_hook_event_at`) over `last_event_at`.

items: `compute_silence()` intentionally prioritizes visible activity sources (`last_jsonl_record_at`, `last_file_event_at`, `last_hook_event_at`) over `last_event_at`.

line: - Added the requested tests as an integration test file at `tests/metrics_tests.rs` instead of modifying the existing internal unit tests in `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:253`.

items: Added the requested tests as an integration test file at `tests/metrics_tests.rs` instead of modifying the existing internal unit tests in `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:253`.

line: - Kept `is_bash_failure()` coverage aligned to the acceptance criteria’s 4 signal classes: `is_error`, non-empty `stderr`, `interrupted`, and content-pattern failure text.

items: Kept `is_bash_failure()` coverage aligned to the acceptance criteria’s 4 signal classes: `is_error`, non-empty `stderr`, `interrupted`, and content-pattern failure text.

line: - The repo already has overlapping in-module unit tests for some of these helpers in `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:274`; this new file duplicates some coverage intentionally to satisfy the task requirement to create `tests/metrics_tests.rs`.
